meta.descriptionTemplate: The export-document pack for UN 1361 coconut charcoal from {{port}}: commercial invoice, B/L, COO, MSDS, DGD, vanning certificate, plus SHT & COA on request.

heroSubtitle: Every UN 1361 shipment travels with a document pack. We issue {{documentsStandardCount}} standard documents with each container and {{documentsAdditionalCount}} more on request.

hero.ctaQuoteAria: Request the document list on WhatsApp

intro.p1: This page sits under the {{brand}} Logistics pillar. It lists the export documents we issue for a coconut charcoal shipment, who issues each one, and what your customs broker uses it for.

keyFacts.heading: Documents at a glance
keyFacts.definition: What ships with your container, and how the originals reach you.

certificate-of-origin.label: COO, Form A and FTA forms

dgdNote.body: The Dangerous Goods Declaration must carry the SP 978 data fields — production date, packing date, and the temperature on the day of packing.

shtNote.body: The Self-Heating Test report is supporting hazard evidence, not a route to non-DG status.

insuranceNote.body: A marine insurance certificate is buyer-arranged evidence of cover.

countryReq.body: Destination requirements vary: GCC and African markets often add chamber-of-commerce and consular legalization; the EU and Australia require a phytosanitary certificate. US entries need ISF data from us up front.
countryReq.rulesLinkLabel: How the incoterm sets the document split

items.q: What export documents do you provide?
items.a: Every shipment travels with {{documentsStandardCount}} standard documents — the commercial invoice, packing list, bill of lading, certificate of origin, MSDS, dangerous goods declaration, container vanning certificate, and the Indonesian export declaration (PEB). A further {{documentsAdditionalCount}} are available on request as paid add-ons, including the Self-Heating Test report and a Certificate of Analysis.

items.q: Do you provide a Self-Heating Test report and a COA?
items.a: Yes, both on request as paid lab add-ons. The SHT report is supporting hazard evidence for the dangerous-goods shipment (it no longer exempts the cargo from DG rules), and the COA independently verifies the burn specifications. Ask for the current lab cost and processing time at quotation.

items.q: Which documents does my country require?
items.a: It depends on the destination. GCC and several African markets require chamber-of-commerce and consular legalization on top of the standard pack; the EU and Australia require a phytosanitary certificate; US entries require ISF data from us before the cargo loads. Tell us your destination and we will confirm the exact pack.
